titel: Report on the use of covert investigative measures during 2024.
html: In the report, the government explains how the provisions in the Code of Criminal Procedure regarding secret interception of electronic communications, secret surveillance of electronic communications, secret camera surveillance, and secret eavesdropping in criminal investigations have been applied during 2024. The government also explains how the Act (2020:62) on secret data access has been applied during the year. The report also includes information on the application of the Act (2007:979) on measures to prevent certain particularly serious crimes, including searches, investigations at other locations, and remote searches. The report also includes an explanation of the collection of information according to the Act (2012:278) on the collection of information about electronic communications in the intelligence activities of law enforcement agencies. Finally, the report covers the use of secret coercive measures in the activities of the Security Police.
tldr: The government has released a report detailing how the police and security services have used covert methods to spy on criminals during the year 2024. The report reveals the extent to which they have employed techniques such as wiretapping and video surveillance to solve crimes.
lattlast:
The government has published a report detailing how authorities have used covert methods to investigate crimes during the year 2024. These methods, referred to as "covert measures," allow the police to use technical tools without the knowledge of the person being monitored.

The report describes the extent to which the police have employed methods such as telephone wiretapping and camera surveillance. It also covers the reading of digital information and the use of hidden cameras in rooms. Furthermore, it outlines the regulations governing how the police are permitted to search for evidence in locations where they do not have permission to be.

The text also explains how intelligence agencies have collected information by monitoring electronic communications. This is a means for the state to gather information about events in society in order to prevent crime.

Finally, the report contains information on how the Swedish Security Service (Säkerhetspolisen), the agency responsible for addressing the most serious threats to Sweden, has utilized these covert methods in its work during the year.
